description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer with a large, rectangular stone or concrete head that appears weathered and roughly textured. The head is solid and blocky, with slightly rounded edges and a visible wooden wedge securing it to the handle. The handle is long, straight, and made of wood, showing a natural grain and a slightly tapered end for grip. The wood is darkened, possibly from age or use, and the surface appears rough and splintered in places. The overall appearance suggests this tool is designed for demolition or breaking hard surfaces, and it shows signs of extensive use and exposure to the elements.
description: A rugged sledgehammer with a large, weathered stone head and a long, rough wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, rectangular face of the stone head.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular shape of the hammer head and the wooden wedge securing the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle and the stone head, with visible texture and wear.
description_view_3: End-on view focusing on the narrow face of the hammer head and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, slightly tapered, rough and splintered with visible grain
attribute_head_type: rectangular sledgehammer head made of stone or concrete, with flat striking faces and rounded edges
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible wear, rough texture, splintering on handle, weathering and chipping on head
